description: A dish of raw seafood cured in citrus juice, most commonly lime or lemon, then mixed with ingredients such as onion, chili, herbs, and sometimes tomato or corn. Different countries prepare it in different styles.
preparation_steps: Use very fresh seafood and cut it into bite-sized pieces.
Marinate the seafood in citrus juice with salt for the desired time.
Add onion, chili, and herbs or other accompaniments.
Serve chilled, often with corn, sweet potato, or crackers depending on the regional style.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low; higher with corn or sweet potato sides

health_advice.benefits: Usually high in protein and relatively light, especially when made mostly from seafood and vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Because the seafood is not heat-cooked, food safety and freshness are very important.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use high-quality seafood from a reliable source and keep it well chilled.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional ceviche is seafood-based. Gluten-free suitability depends on what it is served with.

allergens: Possible gluten if served with crackers or packaged seasonings
